answer:
Salesforce supports two primary authentication methods for its APIs. OAuth 2.0 is the recommended, most secure approach for all API types [1][2][3]. It uses an external client app or connected app to exchange credentials for access tokens [1]. For REST and GraphQL APIs, you provide this access token in the authorization header [4][5].

Alternatively, the SOAP API login call is available [6]. This method authenticates via a username and password (with a security token appended if not on a trusted IP) to return a session ID and server URL [7][6]. Note that login is disabled by default in new organizations [6].
